message:
Lists of structs in capnwasm/dynamic — read parity with codegen

Schema entries can now declare a list of structs:
  field: { kind: "listStruct", slot: N, element: defineSchema({...}) }

The reader walks each element with cpp_any_enter_list_at(i), materializes
all fields per the element schema, then leaves. After every element it
re-opens the outer list defensively, since reading any inner-list field
on the current element would have replaced any_list_reader. Eager
materialization keeps the API safe — you get an array of plain objects
back, not sub-readers that could break on sibling access.

Test uses a hand-crafted Cap'n Proto composite-list message of two
items (id=1, id=2). The wire-format gotcha: the wordCount field in the
list pointer is the total word count of the elements EXCLUDING the tag
word — not including. Cost me one debugging round.

The dynamic reader now covers everything the codegen reader does on the
read side: primitives, lists of primitives, nested structs, lists of
structs. No remaining capability gaps for reads. Builders are still
codegen-only.

139 tests pass (was 135).

files.patch:
@@ -191,7 +191,7 @@ reader.pick(["name", "age"]);   // one wasm round trip, only the fields you ask
 reader.fields.email;            // Proxy access for ergonomic single-field reads
 ```
 
-The descriptor is wire-compatible with what `npx capnwasm gen` emits (`SomeReader._FIELDS`). A build step that strips a generated reader to its `_FIELDS` object can feed it directly to `openDynamic`. Supported field kinds: `text`, `data`, `uint8/16/32`, `int8/16/32`, `int64`, `uint64`, `float32/64`, `bool`, plus `listUint8/16/32/64`, `listInt8/16/32/64`, `listFloat32/64`, `listBool`, `listText`, `listData`, plus nested structs via `{ kind: "struct", slot: N, schema: defineSchema(...) }`. Lists-of-structs aren't in this pass — use codegen for those.
+The descriptor is wire-compatible with what `npx capnwasm gen` emits (`SomeReader._FIELDS`). A build step that strips a generated reader to its `_FIELDS` object can feed it directly to `openDynamic`. Supported field kinds: `text`, `data`, `uint8/16/32`, `int8/16/32`, `int64`, `uint64`, `float32/64`, `bool`, plus `listUint8/16/32/64`, `listInt8/16/32/64`, `listFloat32/64`, `listBool`, `listText`, `listData`, plus `{ kind: "struct", slot, schema }` for nested structs and `{ kind: "listStruct", slot, element }` for lists of structs. Reads cover everything the codegen path does; the dynamic reader is read-only — `buildSomething` builders are still codegen.
 
 When to choose dynamic:

@@ -60,11 +60,16 @@ the wasm-side cursor would be invalidated by sibling access otherwise.
 Null-pointer slots return a default-initialized object (each field at
 its type's default), matching codegen reader semantics.
 
-Still on the list:
-
-- **Lists of structs** — `cpp_any_enter_list_at(i)` is exported; would
-  walk into element i, materialize via the element schema, leave. Same
-  eager-object pattern as nested structs.
+**Update (2026-04-30, fourth pass):** lists of structs landed too. Use
+`{ kind: "listStruct", slot: N, element: defineSchema(...) }`. Each
+element is materialized via the same eager pattern as nested structs.
+The reader re-opens the outer list between elements so any inner-list
+read on an element doesn't disturb the iterator's cursor. Empty list →
+`[]`, matching capnp wire-format defaults.
+
+The dynamic reader now covers everything the codegen path does on the
+read side: primitives, lists of primitives, nested structs, lists of
+structs. No remaining capability gaps for reads.
 
 ## 3. RPC pipelining is implemented but not pipelined under `await`
